---
title: "Surveys"
---

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam, quis nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris nisi ut aliquip ex ea commodo consequat. Duis aute irure dolor in reprehenderit in voluptate velit esse cillum dolore eu fugiat nulla pariatur. Excepteur sint occaecat cupidatat non proident, sunt in culpa qui officia deserunt mollit anim id est laborum



## A working example

The examples below relate to a real-world example of a multi-country survey fielded for a European Horizon research consortium. The aim was to explore citizens' attitudes towards participanting in the policy process, with a special focus on regions potentially afflicted by climate change policies. 

- [Hosting a survey using google forms](https://digipols-admin.github.io/teaching_template/surveys-tab/dust_survey_google.html) 

- [Link to survey support webpage. ](https://fmendez72.github.io/app_dust/){target="_blank"} This website offers an example of an RStudio workflow website and dashboard used to support a research consortium.


## A google approach

Add introduction

- [Example of a google survey form](https://digipols-admin.github.io/teaching_template/surveys-tab/tvhours_google.html)

- [Example of a google looker dahsboard](https://digipols-admin.github.io/teaching_template/surveys-tab/tvhours_looker.html)

Add list of resources


## A Shiny approach

Add introduction about Shiny

Add links to Quarto Shiny integration

- [Link to Shiny questionnaire](https://digipols-admin.github.io/teaching_template/surveys-tab/tvhours_google.html)

- [Links to static Quarto report](https://digipols-admin.github.io/teaching_template/surveys-tab/tvhours_looker.html)
